title: Dynamics_of_Premixed_H2-CH4_Flames_Under_Near_Blowoff_Conditions
text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Da = (τflow / τESR) * (Kext / K)
*   Kext = 1 / TESR

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 2: Schematic of the combustor facility with Mie measurement window and optical probe**
    *   Components:
        *   Exhaust Nozzle
        *   Combustor (Quartz tube)
        *   Mie Scattering
        *   Optical Probe
        *   Centerbody
        *   Swirler

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Kext: extinction strain rate
*   τESR: chemical time scale related to extinction strain rate
*   Kedge: flow strain rate at the flame edge value.
*   0.  5 <    Kedge / Kext < 1
*   Nozzle inner diameter: 28 mm
*   Center body outer diameter: 20 mm
*   Overall flow area: 3.0 cm²

title: Dynamics_of_Premixed_H2-CH4_Flames_Under_Near_Blowoff_Conditions
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   S = (2/3) * [ (1 - (d1/d)3) / (1 - (d1/d)2) ] tan(@)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 3:**
    *   Axes: Time (s), OH'(t) / averaged OH'(t)
    *   Units: seconds for time, no unit for ratio
    *   Curves: Time series data of OH* signal of CH4 flame at equivalence ratios of 0.8 (top), 0.62 (middle), and 0.51 (bottom).
    *   Key values: Time 0 to 5s, OH ratio 0-10

*   **Figure 4:**
    *   Axes: Time (s), OH'(t) / averaged OH'(t)
    *   Units: seconds for time, no unit for ratio
    *   Curve: Time series data of OH signal for methane flame at equivalence ratio of 0.54
    *   Key values:  Local extinction at around 0.2 s, Reignition point marked

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Swirler angle: 45 degrees
*   Theoretical swirl number: 0.85
*   Fuel injected 150 cm upstream
*   Combustor length: 305 mm (12 in.)
*   Combustor inner diameter: 115 mm
*   Combustor outer diameter: 120 mm
*   Exhaust nozzle contraction area ratio: 0.44
*   Exhaust nozzle length: 102 mm
*   Exhaust nozzle inner diameter: 51 mm
*   Maximum uncertainty in ratios: 0.01-0.02
*   Largest uncertainty in phi: 0.03 (with pure CH4)
*   Nozzle exit velocity: 33 m/s
*   Combustor pressure: 1.0 atm
*   Reactant temperature: 300 K
*   Aluminum oxide particle size: 3 μm
*   Laser pulse beam energy: 120 mJ/pulse
*   Laser wavelength: 532 nm
*   Focal lengths: f=1 m (spherical lens), f=25.4 mm (cylindrical lens)
*   Light Sheet Thickness: 1 mm
*   CCD camera resolution: 1600x1200 pixels (52x39 mm^2)
*   Laser shot duration: 5 μs
*   Laser line filter: 532 nm, FWHM of 3 nm
*   Optical fiber NA: 0.44
*   Probe location: 46 mm above, 171 mm radially
*   Filter center: 308 nm
*   Filter width: 10 nm
*   PMT bandwidth: 20 kHz
*   Sampling frequency: 2 kHz
*   Low pass filter frequency: 1 kHz

title: Dynamics_of_Premixed_H2-CH4_Flames_Under_Near_Blowoff_Conditions
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   TESR=1/Kext
*   D/Uo (for Damkohler number)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 5: Dependence of local extinction event frequency on equivalence ratio of CH4/H2 flames**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Equivalence Ratio (unitless)
        *   y-axis: Events/Second
    *   Curves:
        *   Three curves representing:
            *   0% H2
            *   20% H2
            *   50% H2
    *   Key Values: Equivalence ratio ranges from 0.3 to 0.8,  Event/second ranges from 0 to 7.

*   **Fig. 6: Dependence of Kext, blowoff limits, and event rate on percentage of hydrogen and equivalence ratio.**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis:   %H2
        *   y-axis:  Kext
    *   Curves:
        *   Kext (decreasing)
        *   LBO (Blowoff Limit)
        *   1 event/s
        *   2 events/s
        *   3 events/s
    *   Key Values: %H2 goes from 0 to 70,  Kext ranges from 0.3 to 0.7.

*   **Fig. 7: Dependence of extinction event rate upon Damköhler number of CH4/H2 flames**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis:  Damkohler Number
        *   y-axis:  Events/Second
    *   Curves:
        *   Three curves representing:
            *   0% H2
            *   20% H2
            *   50% H2
    *   Key Values: Damkohler Number ranges from 0.1 to 10,  Events/Second ranges from 0 to 3.5

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Equivalence ratio decremented over steps of 0.01.
*   Data taken at each point for 30 s.
*   1400 1/s, 1200 1/s, 800 1/s, 400 1/s, and 200 1/s extinction strain rates.
*   0/100%, 20/80%, 40/60%, 50/50%, and 75/25% H2/CH4 mixtures.
*   Equivalence ratio steps of 0.02.
*   Event counts of 1 events/s, 2 events/s, and 3 events/s corresponding to Da=0.5, 0.35, and 0.2, respectively.
*   0.3 and 0.5 as thresholds
*   Timescale from O(1s) to O(0.001s).

title: Dynamics_of_Premixed_H2-CH4_Flames_Under_Near_Blowoff_Conditions
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ((s) = integral from -infinity to -kext- of P(κ,s) dκ + integral from kext+ to infinity of P(κ,s) dκ  (3)
*   P(κ) = exp[-(κ - κ)^2 / (2σ^2)] / (σ * sqrt(2π)) (4)
*   Da(s) = κext / κ = 1 +  (σ_κ(s) / (κ(s) * sqrt(2)) ) * erf^-1[1 - 2ζ(s)] (5)
*   ζ(s) = 1 - erf[ (Da(s) - 1) / (2σ_κ(s)/κ(s))  ] (6)

**2. Graphs:**

*   **Figure 8:** Comparison of three chemical time scales at fuel/air ratios associated with event rate of 1 event/s
    *   X-axis: % H2 (percentage of hydrogen)
    *   Y-axis: Time scale (s)
    *   Curves:
        *   alpha/S^2
        *   tau_PSR
        *   1/kappa_ext
*   **Figure 9:**  Notional description of time variation of local Damkohler number at given point on flame sheet, illustrating local extinction events at two average Damköhler number values, Da, one farther (top) and closer (bottom) to blowoff.
    *   X-axis: Time
    *   Y-axis: Damkohler #
    *   Curves: Damkohler # vs Time
    *   Average Da value is marked on the y axis
    *   Extinction value is marked on the y axis

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Da: Damköhler number
*   κ:  Actual strain rate
*   σ_κ: Strain fluctuation amplitude
*   ζ: Fraction of time the flame is extinguished
*   κ_ext: Extinction strain rate
*   P(κ, s): Probability density function of the flame strain rate
*   Kext-: Negative Extinction strained rates
*   Kext+: Positive Extinction strained rates
*   Kref: reference strained rate

title: Dynamics_of_Premixed_H2-CH4_Flames_Under_Near_Blowoff_Conditions
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the breakdown of technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

    P(x) Probability of a hole being present at a given axial location, x per unit axial distance.
    Note: time in the viewing window (~20 mm/30 m/s =0.67 ms)
**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 10:**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: PDF of Strain Rate, P(x)
        *   x-axis: Flame Strain Rate, κ
    *   Curves: Representing shapes of PDF related to Kref (reference strain rate), and Kext (extinction strain rate).
*   **Figure 12:**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: Extinction Fraction /mm
        *   x-axis: Axial Location (mm)
    *   Curves:
        *   Data points for CH4 (filled squares) at equivalence ratio 0.62.
        *   Data points for 50/50 H2/CH4/O (open circles) at equivalence ratio 0.43.
        *   Solid Line: Represents calculated result assuming holes are generated uniformly and propagate downstream.

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 11:** Flame front in raw Mie scattering images
    *   Components/Labels: There are colored Vorticity scales on the figures. There aren't any labels for the components of the flame. The images show raw Mie scattering images, instantaneous isovorticity field and flame front.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Flame propagation speed: 30 m/s
*   Threshold level for density gradients: 40%
*   Ay=1mm
*   Equivalence ratio (Fig. 11): ~0.61
*   Equivalence ratio (Fig. 12): 0.62 (CH4) / 0.43 (H2/CH4/O)
